subject: Physics
description: Master the fundamentals of electricity! This worksheet covers current, potential difference, EMF, resistance, Ohm's Law, and circuit analysis. Pay attention to definitions and how formulas link to concepts. Show all working for calculations!

questions.question: Electric current is defined as the rate of flow of electric charge. Which formula correctly represents this definition?

Based on the definition I = Q/t, what is the SI unit of current?

Hint: Charge is measured in coulombs (C) and time in seconds (s).
options: Ampere (A), which is coulombs per second (C/s)
questions.answer: Ampere (A), which is coulombs per second (C/s)

questions.question: Potential difference (p.d.) is defined as the work done per unit charge in moving charge between two points. Which formula represents this definition?

From the definition V = W/Q:
• V = potential difference
• W = work done (energy transferred)
• Q = charge

What is the SI unit of potential difference?
options: Volt (V), which is joules per coulomb (J/C)
questions.answer: Volt (V), which is joules per coulomb (J/C)

questions.question: Electromotive force (e.m.f.) is defined as the energy converted from other forms to electrical energy per unit charge. How does this differ from potential difference?
options: EMF refers to energy supplied by a source, while p.d. refers to energy used by a component
EMF and p.d. are exactly the same thing
EMF is measured in amperes, p.d. in volts
EMF only applies to alternating current
questions.answer: EMF refers to energy supplied by a source, while p.d. refers to energy used by a component

questions.question: Resistance is the opposition to the flow of current in a circuit. According to Ohm's Law, V = IR. Rearranging for R gives:

From I = Q/t, we can rearrange to get Q = It.

A current of 2 A flows through a wire for 30 seconds. How much charge flows through the wire?

Q = I × t = 2 × 30

A charge of 180 C flows through a circuit in 1 minute. What is the current?

Remember: 1 minute = 60 seconds
I = Q/t = 180/60

A lamp draws a current of 0.5 A. How long will it take for 150 C of charge to flow through the lamp?

Rearrange Q = It to find t:
t = Q/I = 150/0.5

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 1: Current and Charge ⭐ MAIN QUESTION
questions.question:
In a circuit, 2.4 × 10³ C of charge passes through a resistor in 20 minutes. Calculate the current flowing through the resistor.

Step 1: Convert 20 minutes to seconds = 20 × 60 = 1200 s
Step 2: I = Q/t = 2400/1200

From the definition V = W/Q:

60 J of energy is used to move 20 C of charge through a resistor. What is the potential difference across the resistor?

V = W/Q = 60/20

The potential difference across a component is 12 V. If 5 C of charge flows through it, how much energy is transferred?

Rearrange V = W/Q to find W:
W = V × Q = 12 × 5

A battery transfers 450 J of energy when 30 C of charge passes through it. What is the e.m.f. of the battery?

e.m.f. = W/Q = 450/30

We know:
• Q = It (charge = current × time)
• V = W/Q (potential difference = work/charge)

Substituting Q = It into V = W/Q gives:
V = W/It

Rearranging for W:
W = ?

A heater operates at 240 V and draws a current of 5 A for 2 minutes. Using W = VIt, calculate the energy transferred.

Step 1: Convert 2 minutes = 120 s
Step 2: W = V × I × t = 240 × 5 × 120

Ohm's Law states that the current through a conductor is directly proportional to the potential difference across it, provided temperature remains constant.

V = IR

If V = 12 V and I = 3 A, what is R?

A resistor of 6 Ω has a potential difference of 18 V across it. What current flows through it?

I = V/R = 18/6

A current of 0.4 A flows through a 25 Ω resistor. What is the potential difference across it?

V = I × R = 0.4 × 25
The graph shows that current is directly proportional to voltage for an ohmic conductor. If the voltage doubles, what happens to the current?
From V = IR, if the resistance of a circuit doubles while the voltage remains constant, what happens to the current?

A 12 V battery is connected to a lamp with resistance 8 Ω. Calculate:
(a) The current through the lamp
(b) The charge flowing through the lamp in 1 minute

I = V/R = 12/8 = 1.5 A
Q = It = 1.5 × 60 = ?

A resistor is connected to a 9 V battery. When 540 C of charge flows through the resistor in 3 minutes, calculate the resistance of the resistor.

Step 1: Find current: I = Q/t = 540/(3×60) = 540/180 = 3 A
Step 2: Find resistance: R = V/I = 9/3
In a series circuit, components are connected one after another in a single loop. Which statement about current in a series circuit is TRUE?
options: Current is the same through all components
Current is greatest at the positive terminal
questions.answer: Current is the same through all components

questions.question:
In a series circuit with a 12 V battery and three bulbs:
• The current is the same through all bulbs

What about the potential difference (voltage)?
options: Voltage is the same across all bulbs
Total voltage equals the battery voltage, and it divides among the bulbs
Voltage has no meaning in series circuits
questions.answer: Total voltage equals the battery voltage, and it divides among the bulbs

questions.question:
For resistors in SERIES, the total resistance is the sum of individual resistances:

R_total = R₁ + R₂ + R₃ + ...

Three resistors of 4 Ω, 6 Ω, and 10 Ω are connected in series. What is the total resistance?

Two resistors of 8 Ω and 4 Ω are connected in series to a 24 V battery.

Step 1: Total resistance = 8 + 4 = 12 Ω
Step 2: Current = V/R = 24/12 = ?

What is the current in the circuit?

From the previous question: Two resistors (8 Ω and 4 Ω) in series, current = 2 A.

Using V = IR, what is the potential difference across the 8 Ω resistor?

V = I × R = 2 × 8

Continuing: What is the potential difference across the 4 Ω resistor?

V = I × R = 2 × 4

Let's verify: In a series circuit, the sum of p.d. across all components should equal the e.m.f.

P.d. across 8 Ω resistor = 16 V
P.d. across 4 Ω resistor = 8 V
Sum = 16 + 8 = ?

Does this equal the battery voltage of 24 V?

Three resistors of 5 Ω, 10 Ω, and 15 Ω are connected in series to a 15 V battery. Calculate the potential difference across the 10 Ω resistor.

Step 1: Total R = 5 + 10 + 15 = 30 Ω
Step 2: Current I = V/R = 15/30 = 0.5 A
Step 3: P.d. across 10 Ω = I × R = 0.5 × 10
In a parallel circuit, components are connected in separate branches. Which statement about VOLTAGE in a parallel circuit is TRUE?
options: Voltage is the same across all branches
Voltage is zero in all branches
Voltage is greatest in the longest branch
questions.answer: Voltage is the same across all branches

questions.question:
In a parallel circuit with a 12 V battery:
• Voltage is the same (12 V) across all branches

What about the current?
options: Current is the same in all branches
Total current divides among the branches, and the sum of branch currents equals total current
Current only flows in one branch
questions.answer: Total current divides among the branches, and the sum of branch currents equals total current

questions.question:
For resistors in PARALLEL:

1/R_total = 1/R₁ + 1/R₂ + ...

Two resistors of 6 Ω each are connected in parallel. What is the total resistance?

1/R = 1/6 + 1/6 = 2/6 = 1/3
R = 3 Ω

Important concept: When resistors are connected in parallel, the total resistance is always LESS than the smallest individual resistance.

Two resistors of 4 Ω and 12 Ω are in parallel.
1/R = 1/4 + 1/12 = 3/12 + 1/12 = 4/12 = 1/3
R = 3 Ω

Is 3 Ω less than the smallest resistor (4 Ω)?

Two resistors of 10 Ω and 15 Ω are connected in parallel to a 12 V battery.

First, find the current through the 10 Ω resistor.
Since voltage is the same across parallel branches:
I = V/R = 12/10

From the previous question: Two resistors (10 Ω and 15 Ω) in parallel, 12 V supply.

The current through the 10 Ω resistor is 1.2 A.
Find the current through the 15 Ω resistor.

I = V/R = 12/15 = 0.8 A

What is the TOTAL current from the battery?

Let's verify using total resistance:

10 Ω and 15 Ω in parallel:
1/R = 1/10 + 1/15 = 3/30 + 2/30 = 5/30 = 1/6
R_total = 6 Ω

Total current = V/R_total = 12/6 = 2 A

Does this match our previous calculation (1.2 + 0.8 = 2 A)?

Three resistors of 12 Ω, 12 Ω, and 6 Ω are connected in parallel to an 18 V battery. Calculate the total current from the battery.

1/R = 1/12 + 1/12 + 1/6 = 1/12 + 1/12 + 2/12 = 4/12 = 1/3
R_total = 3 Ω
I = V/R = 18/3

A mixed circuit contains both series and parallel combinations. To find total resistance, work step by step:

In this circuit:
• R₁ (4 Ω) is in series with a parallel combination of R₂ (6 Ω) and R₃ (3 Ω)

First, find the equivalent resistance of R₂ and R₃ in parallel:
1/R = 1/6 + 1/3 = 1/6 + 2/6 = 3/6 = 1/2
R_parallel = ?

From the previous question:
• R₁ = 4 Ω (in series)
• R₂ and R₃ in parallel = 2 Ω

Now find the total resistance of the circuit.
Since 4 Ω is in SERIES with 2 Ω:
R_total = 4 + 2 = ?

The circuit has total resistance 6 Ω connected to a 12 V battery.

What is the current flowing from the battery?
I = V/R = 12/6

In the circuit:
• R₁ (4 Ω) is in series
• R₂ (6 Ω) and R₃ (3 Ω) are in parallel
• Total current from battery = 2 A

The current through R₁ is 2 A (series - same current).

What is the potential difference across R₁?
V = IR = 2 × 4

From the previous question:
• Battery voltage = 12 V
• P.d. across R₁ = 8 V

The remaining voltage appears across the parallel combination (R₂ and R₃).
P.d. across parallel section = 12 - 8 = 4 V

What is the current through R₃ (3 Ω)?
I = V/R = 4/3

Let's verify: The currents through R₂ and R₃ should add up to 2 A (the total current).

Current through R₃ = 4/3 ≈ 1.33 A
Current through R₂ = 4/6 ≈ 0.67 A

1.33 + 0.67 = ?

A circuit consists of:
• R₁ (8 Ω) and R₂ (8 Ω) in parallel
• This parallel combination is in series with R₃ (6 Ω)
• Connected to a 20 V battery

Step 1: R₁ ∥ R₂ = (8×8)/(8+8) = 64/16 = 4 Ω
Step 2: Total R = 4 + 6 = 10 Ω
Step 3: Current = 20/10 = 2 A

What is the p.d. across R₃?
Two identical bulbs are connected in SERIES to a battery. One bulb blows (breaks). What happens to the other bulb?
options: It goes out (no current can flow)
questions.answer: It goes out (no current can flow)

questions.question: Two identical bulbs are connected in PARALLEL to a battery. One bulb blows. What happens to the other bulb?

A circuit has two resistors in SERIES connected to a fixed voltage supply. An identical third resistor is added in series.

What happens to:
(a) Total resistance - INCREASES
(b) Total current from the supply - ?

A circuit has two resistors in PARALLEL connected to a fixed voltage supply. An identical third resistor is added in parallel.

What happens to:
(a) Total resistance - DECREASES
(b) Total current from the supply - ?

A circuit contains a 12 V battery connected to two identical bulbs A and B in series. A third identical bulb C is then connected in PARALLEL with bulb B.

Compared to before, what happens to:
• Bulb A: Gets brighter (more current flows)
• Bulb B: Gets dimmer (shares current with C)

What happens to the TOTAL current from the battery?
